column28: Are you aware of any initiatives or programs aimed at promoting sustainable fishing practices in your community?
no-take zones, managed access, size limit, zoning, licensing, enforcement, marine protected areas, close seassons
Capacity building in alternative livelihoods, diversification and sustainable fishing practices
Alternative or livelihood diversification, trainning done by SEA
I work for TIDE, we do protected area fisheries and ecosystem management in the Souther Barrier Reef Complex, as well as coastal ecosystems
At the moment Im unaware of such programs
I do not eat shrimp and only purchase fresh fish in Belize from fishers. There is a community initiatives and training through Placencia town guide association and Fragments of Hope, SEA, BTIA, BTB

our local fishers are careful of the size they bring home. Too small and very large are put back in the sea. Our tradition is that the large ones are aggers that should remain to populate the stock. Avoid destroying habitat like corals, mangroves etc.
